---
title: "IgG Levels by Vaccine Dose and Age"
author: "Stuart Torres, Ben Arnold"
date: "Updated `r Sys.Date()`"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
    code-fold: true
    link-external-newwindow: true
---

# Preamble

```{r preamble, message=FALSE}
library(here)
source(here("R", "0-config.R"))
```

# Load and prepare data

```{r load-data}
#-------------------------------
# read processed dataset
#-------------------------------
d0 <- read_rds(here("data", "ecomid_sero_vpd_2026-06-11.rds"))

#-------------------------------
# drop the 2 rows with missing
# log10mfi (no antibody value)
#-------------------------------
n_drop_mfi <- sum(is.na(d0$log10mfi))
d0 <- d0 %>% filter(!is.na(log10mfi))
cat("Rows dropped for missing log10mfi:", n_drop_mfi, "\n")

#-------------------------------
# FLAG: implausible age(s). One sample has a negative age — the visit date
# precedes the recorded DOB, so DOB or visit date is mis-entered. The age
# axis below is limited to 6-24 months, which hides it; it is NOT removed
# from the data pending review of the age calculation in script 1.
#-------------------------------
age_flags <- d0 %>%
  filter(agemonths_best < 5) %>%
  distinct(childid, timepoint, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  select(sampleid, childid, timepoint, agedays_best, agemonths_best, dob, visitdate_best)
cat("\nFLAGGED implausible-age child-visits (agemonths_best < 5):\n")
print(as.data.frame(age_flags), row.names = FALSE)

#-------------------------------
# custom log10 axis labels
# (convention from draft scripts)
#-------------------------------
log10labs <- c(
  expression(10^0),
  expression(10^1),
  expression(10^2),
  expression(10^3),
  expression(10^4)
)

#-------------------------------
# seropositivity thresholds (MFI-bg) pulled from the data
#-------------------------------
thresholds <- d0 %>%
  distinct(pathogen, mficut) %>%
  deframe()
print(thresholds)

#-------------------------------
# dose-level color maps (shared with Fig 1B via 0-config.R).
# Dose 0 (unvaccinated) is gray; doses 1-3 use high-contrast Dark2 hues —
# warm (gold/orange/magenta) for pentavalent, cool (teal/purple) for MMR.
#-------------------------------
penta_cols <- penta_pal   # names "0".."3": gray + 3 warm hues
mmr_cols   <- mmr_pal     # names "0".."2": gray + 2 cool hues
```

```{r panel-helpers}
#-------------------------------
# helper: jittered points + boxplot (drawn on top) of log10 IgG by cumulative
# dose. Boxplots are unfilled (fill = NA) so points show through, narrow, and
# layered over the jitter. (Used 4x across the two figures.)
#-------------------------------
make_dose_panel <- function(dat, dose_var, threshold, dose_cols, xlab, ylab, ylim) {
  dat <- dat %>% mutate(dose_f = factor(.data[[dose_var]]))
  ggplot(dat, aes(x = dose_f, y = log10mfi)) +
    geom_jitter(aes(color = dose_f), width = 0.15, height = 0, alpha = 0.25) +
    geom_boxplot(outliers = FALSE, fill = NA, width = 0.3, color = "gray20") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = log10(threshold), linetype = "dashed") +
    scale_color_manual(values = dose_cols, name = "Doses received", drop = FALSE) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = 0:4, labels = log10labs) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = ylim) +
    labs(x = xlab, y = ylab) +
    # legend keys at full opacity even though plotted points are semi-transparent
    guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(alpha = 1, size = 2.5))) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
}

#-------------------------------
# helper: scatter of log10 IgG by exact age (colored by cumulative dose), with
# an unfilled boxplot at each target visit age (grouped by timepoint) layered on
# top to summarize the IgG distribution. Age axis limited to 6-24 months.
#-------------------------------
make_age_panel <- function(dat, dose_var, threshold, dose_cols, ylab, ylim) {
  dat <- dat %>% mutate(dose_f = factor(.data[[dose_var]]))
  ggplot(dat, aes(x = agemonths_best, y = log10mfi)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = dose_f), alpha = 0.25) +
    geom_boxplot(aes(x = timepoint, y = log10mfi, group = timepoint),
                 inherit.aes = FALSE, outliers = FALSE, fill = NA,
                 width = 1.5, color = "gray20") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = log10(threshold), linetype = "dashed") +
    scale_color_manual(values = dose_cols, name = "Doses received", drop = FALSE) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(6, 24, by = 3)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = 0:4, labels = log10labs) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(5, 25), ylim = ylim) +
    labs(x = "Age at collection (months)", y = ylab) +
    guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(alpha = 1, size = 2.5))) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
}
```

---

# Figure 4: Pentavalent vaccine — IgG by dose and age (tetanus, diphtheria)

```{r fig4-data}
#-------------------------------
# tetanus and diphtheria; cumulative pentavalent doses
# drop rows with missing penta_doses
#-------------------------------
d_tet  <- d0 %>% filter(pathogen == "Tetanus",    !is.na(penta_doses))
d_diph <- d0 %>% filter(pathogen == "Diphtheria", !is.na(penta_doses))

cat("Tetanus rows (penta_doses non-missing):",    nrow(d_tet),
    "| dropped:", sum(d0$pathogen == "Tetanus")    - nrow(d_tet), "\n")
cat("Diphtheria rows (penta_doses non-missing):", nrow(d_diph),
    "| dropped:", sum(d0$pathogen == "Diphtheria") - nrow(d_diph), "\n")

# common y-axis range across both pathogens in this figure
penta_ylim <- range(c(d_tet$log10mfi, d_diph$log10mfi))
```

```{r fig4-plot, fig.width=9, fig.height=8, warning=FALSE}
pA <- make_dose_panel(d_tet, "penta_doses", thresholds["Tetanus"],
                      penta_cols, "Pentavalent doses received",
                      "Tetanus IgG (MFI-bg)", penta_ylim)
pB <- make_age_panel(d_tet, "penta_doses", thresholds["Tetanus"],
                     penta_cols, NULL, penta_ylim)
pC <- make_dose_panel(d_diph, "penta_doses", thresholds["Diphtheria"],
                      penta_cols, "Pentavalent doses received",
                      "Diphtheria IgG (MFI-bg)", penta_ylim)
pD <- make_age_panel(d_diph, "penta_doses", thresholds["Diphtheria"],
                     penta_cols, NULL, penta_ylim)

fig4 <- (pA | pB) / (pC | pD) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold"))

fig4

ggsave(
  filename = here("output", "figures", "fig4_IgG_dose_age_pentavalent.png"),
  plot = fig4,
  width = 200, height = 180, units = "mm", dpi = 300
)
cat("Figure 4 saved.\n")
```

---

# Figure 5: MMR vaccine — IgG by dose and age (rubella, measles)

```{r fig5-data}
#-------------------------------
# rubella and measles; cumulative MMR doses
# drop rows with missing mmr_doses
#-------------------------------
d_rub <- d0 %>% filter(pathogen == "Rubella", !is.na(mmr_doses))
d_mea <- d0 %>% filter(pathogen == "Measles", !is.na(mmr_doses))

cat("Rubella rows (mmr_doses non-missing):", nrow(d_rub),
    "| dropped:", sum(d0$pathogen == "Rubella") - nrow(d_rub), "\n")
cat("Measles rows (mmr_doses non-missing):", nrow(d_mea),
    "| dropped:", sum(d0$pathogen == "Measles") - nrow(d_mea), "\n")

# confirm MMR dose levels present (spec: confirm whether dose = 3 exists)
cat("\nMMR dose levels present:\n")
print(sort(unique(c(d_rub$mmr_doses, d_mea$mmr_doses))))

# common y-axis range across both pathogens in this figure
mmr_ylim <- range(c(d_rub$log10mfi, d_mea$log10mfi))
```

```{r fig5-plot, fig.width=9, fig.height=8, warning=FALSE}
qA <- make_dose_panel(d_rub, "mmr_doses", thresholds["Rubella"],
                      mmr_cols, "MMR doses received",
                      "Rubella IgG (MFI-bg)", mmr_ylim)
qB <- make_age_panel(d_rub, "mmr_doses", thresholds["Rubella"],
                     mmr_cols, NULL, mmr_ylim)
qC <- make_dose_panel(d_mea, "mmr_doses", thresholds["Measles"],
                      mmr_cols, "MMR doses received",
                      "Measles IgG (MFI-bg)", mmr_ylim)
qD <- make_age_panel(d_mea, "mmr_doses", thresholds["Measles"],
                     mmr_cols, NULL, mmr_ylim)

fig5 <- (qA | qB) / (qC | qD) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold"))

fig5

ggsave(
  filename = here("output", "figures", "fig5_IgG_dose_age_MMR.png"),
  plot = fig5,
  width = 200, height = 180, units = "mm", dpi = 300
)
cat("Figure 5 saved.\n")
```

---

# Session information

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
